title: forest_fire
description: A lightning-caused forest fire grows into a large complex threatening a wilderness area and a nearby town.

timeline.title: Lightning Strike Detected
timeline.description: Lightning detection system records a strike in a remote forest sector. Confirm smoke report.

timeline.title: Fire Growth Confirmed
timeline.description: Aerial reconnaissance confirms a 20-acre fire with active spread. Establish Incident Command.

timeline.title: Evacuation Warning Issued
timeline.description: Fire projected to reach adjacent community in 48 hours. Issue evacuation warning for Zone A.

timeline.title: Air Tanker Operations
timeline.description: Air tankers making retardant drops on the fire's flank. Monitor effectiveness.

timeline.title: Evacuation Order Escalated
timeline.description: Fire behavior changes. Upgrade evacuation warning to mandatory order for Zone A and B.

timeline.title: Containment Achieved
timeline.description: Fire crews establish containment lines. Fire is 40% contained. Maintain patrol.

node-1.title: Smoke Confirmed in Wilderness
node-1.situation: A lightning strike last night has produced a confirmed smoke column in a remote forested area. The fire is estimated at 10 acres. Aerial reconnaissance is available.
node-1.question: How do you respond to the initial report?

options.label: A
options.text: Dispatch an initial attack crew via helicopter and establish an Incident Command Post.

options.label: B
options.text: Monitor the fire from aerial reconnaissance for another 24 hours before committing resources.

options.label: C
options.text: Issue a containment plan but wait for the fire to be visible from the road before acting.

node-2.title: Fire Grows Rapidly
node-2.situation: The fire has grown to 500 acres overnight due to low humidity and shifting winds. A nearby town of 2,000 residents is within the projected fire path.
node-2.question: What protective action do you take for the community?

options.label: A
options.text: Issue an evacuation warning for Zone A and alert the emergency broadcast system.

options.label: B
options.text: Wait until the fire is within 10 miles of the town before issuing any public notification.

node-3.title: Air Tanker Strategy
node-3.situation: Air tankers are available but limited to 6 drops per day. The fire has two flanks and a head. The head is advancing most rapidly.
node-3.question: Where do you prioritize air tanker drops?

options.label: A
options.text: Focus drops on the advancing head to slow its progress toward the community.

options.label: B
options.text: Split drops evenly across head and flanks to work toward full containment.

node-4.title: Evacuation Escalation
node-4.situation: The fire's rate of spread has doubled due to an unexpected wind event. Zone A residents are evacuating but Zone B was not yet warned. The fire could reach Zone B in hours.
node-4.question: What is your decision?

options.label: A
options.text: Immediately upgrade Zone B to a mandatory evacuation order and open additional evacuation routes.

options.label: B
options.text: Keep Zone B on a watch status to avoid panic and traffic congestion.

node-5.title: Scenario Complete
node-5.situation: Both evacuation zones have been cleared and containment lines are holding. Air operations have slowed the fire's advance and the community is safe.
node-5.question: Scenario complete. Well done!
